meaning: n.反启蒙主义者adj.反启蒙主义者的
examples: I think that a lot of poetry published today is obscurantist nonsense.
When the psychologist attempts to employ statistical methods her encounters the usual obscurantist notion that you can prver anything with statistics.
[color=#000000]Along with the obscurantist language, bribe-taking culture around the world often involves the avoidance of physically handing the money from one person to another.
The delight some scientists take with obscurantist language should not blind us to the benefits of genomics, but it is likely to do so.
